summary: First comprehensive test run of Singularity money transfer + crypto trading app. Backend API: 78% pass rate (7/9 tests passed). Frontend UI: All auth screens working correctly. Auth registration blocked by Supabase rate limits (external limitation). Core functionality verified: health check, crypto prices, currencies, wallet packages, bot auth requirement. UI fully responsive with proper error handling.

critical.issue: Supabase rate limit exceeded - 'email rate limit exceeded'. Multiple registration attempts hit Supabase free tier rate limit. Auth endpoint itself works (proper error codes returned), but cannot test successful registration flow.
critical.root_cause: External Supabase API rate limiting, not a code bug. Tested with multiple email formats (@test.com, @example.com, @gmail.com) - all hit rate limit after 3-4 attempts.

action_items: Supabase rate limit issue: Wait for rate limit reset (typically 1 hour) or use Supabase paid tier for production
Full end-to-end testing (home, wallet, trade, bot, profile tabs) requires a successfully authenticated user - blocked by rate limit
Consider adding seed/demo user credentials for testing purposes to bypass registration
critical_code_review_comments: MINOR: server.py line 25 has default value for DB_NAME - should fail fast per guidelines: `db = client[os.environ.get('DB_NAME', 'singularity_db')]`
MINOR: Lines 28-37 in server.py use empty string defaults for SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY, STRIPE_API_KEY, EMERGENT_LLM_KEY instead of failing fast
NOTE: All env vars are currently set in .env files, so not breaking in practice
POSITIVE: MongoDB _id properly excluded from all responses using {'_id': 0} projection
POSITIVE: HTTP status codes correctly used (400, 401, 404, 500)
POSITIVE: All React Native components correctly used (View, Text, TouchableOpacity, TextInput)
POSITIVE: All interactive elements have testID attributes for automation
POSITIVE: API URLs from environment variables in frontend (EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL)
POSITIVE: Proper error state handling in frontend with user-friendly error messages

success_rate.backend: 78% (7/9 passed, 2 failed due to external Supabase rate limit)
success_rate.frontend: 100% of auth screens tested - all working correctly

seed_data_creation: No seed data created - registration blocked by Supabase rate limits
context_for_next_testing_agent: Supabase has rate limits on registration. For full E2E testing of authenticated screens (home, wallet, trade, bot, profile), either: 1) Wait 1 hour for rate limit reset, 2) Use different Supabase project, or 3) Have main agent add a demo/seed user with known credentials. Backend health, crypto prices, currencies, and wallet packages endpoints all work correctly. Frontend renders perfectly with proper error handling.
rca_of_issue:
RCA: Supabase Rate Limiting

Reproduction:
1. Attempt to register new user via POST /api/auth/register
2. After 3-4 registration attempts within short time window, Supabase returns 400: 'email rate limit exceeded'
3. Tried multiple email formats: @test.com, @example.com, @gmail.com - all hit rate limit

Root Cause:
- Supabase free tier has rate limits on auth operations
- Multiple test runs triggered rate limiter
- This is an external API limitation, not application code bug

Mitigation:
- For development/testing: Use Supabase paid tier or wait for rate limit reset
- For CI/CD: Use mock auth service or dedicated test Supabase project
- Alternative: Seed database with test users and use login instead of registration for tests

Verification:
- Auth endpoint code is correct (returns proper status codes, validation works)
- Error is properly handled and displayed in frontend UI
- Backend logs show proper error handling
